core_mechanism: linear (Lyapunov) spectral stability analysis of a self-similar fixed point/attractor: linearize the dynamics about the invariant solution and classify perturbation modes by the sign of the eigenvalues of the linearized operator (negative = stable, positive = unstable, zero = marginal modes along a continuous symmetry manifold), with a universal Laplacian eigenvalue problem as the leading operator
brief_reason: The abstract computes exact perturbation spectra (λ=cot²>0 unstable, λ_m=-sec²<0 stable) about self-similar ensemble solutions, identifies tangent zero modes along a symmetry manifold, and finds the leading stability operator to be a universal angular Laplacian — i.e. eigenvalue-sign classification of linearized perturbations around a fixed point.
